MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-23-2016 Off Calendar

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-23-2016 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On May-24-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. AARON S. GORFEIN, ESQ. of Law Office of Elzabeth F. McDonald, Tel No. 415-512-7788 appearing for Plaintiff Mark Barnes;
Attys. HEATHER M. SAGER, ESQ. and AYSE KUZUCUOGLU, ESQ. of Vedder Price, Tel No. 415-749-9500 appearing for Defendants Dena Global Inc and Ngmoco LLC.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS DENA GLOBAL INC. AND NGMOCO LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is denied in its entirety. There is a triable issue whether plaintiff engaged in protected activity to support his claims. There is evidence that plaintiff expressly complained about discrimination and that his doing so was based on his reasonable and good faith belief that what he was opposing constituted unlawful employer conduct. Plaintiff was not obliged to use any "specific legal terms or buzzword to oppose the discrimination (Miller v. Dept. of Corrections (2005) 36 Cal.4th 446, 473-474; Rope v. Auto-Chlor System of Washington, Inc. (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 635, 652.) Paragraphs 25-26 and 31-32 of plaintiff's declaration show that he expressly complained about Mr. Noda's "discrimination." Plaintiff's August 8, 2014 e-mail to Ms. Merkadeau expressly complains about the discrimination. (Merkadeau dec., Ex. 20). Defendants met their burden of showing that the decision to terminate plaintiff's employment was based on legitimate business reasons. The deposition of Mr. Asako shows that he made the decision because plaintiff did not complete his assignments and was not working with other members of the executive team. (Asako Depo., 64:15-65:5). Plaintiff presents substantial evidence creating a triable issue whether the reasons for the termination were pretextual and whether there is a causal nexus between the protected activity and the termination of plaintiff's. The temporal proximity of plaintiff's complaint regarding discrimination and the termination is probative. (Plaintiff's dec., pars. 31 and 36). Mr. Asako accused plaintiff of not being on the same page as Mr. Kondo, but Mr. Kondo never criticized plaintiff. (Plaintiff's dec., par. 32). Mr. Asako stated that the discrimination complaint was going to get him fired and it reflected badly on him. (Plaintiff's dec., par. 32). Mr. Watanabe, who was hostile to the discrimination investigation, recommended the termination to Mr. Asako. (Plaintiff's dec., par. 31; Gorfein Dec., Ex. 5, Watanabe Depo., 146:4-147:3). Plaintiff did not receive any negative criticism regarding his performance prior to the termination. (Plaintiff's dec., par. 36). A reasonable trier of fact could conclude that the termination of plaintiff's employment was based on protected activity rather than plaintiff's work performance because of the hostility to the investigation, the timing of the termination, and the lack of criticism of plaintiff's work prior to the termination. Defendants' objection to paragraphs 25-26, 31-32 and 36 of plaintiff's declaration are overruled. Parties shall meet and confer regarding the language of the proposed order. The proposed order may modify the language of the Court's ruling to clarify that the Court is making no findings of fact, but, merely finding that there is evidence of a triable issue of fact. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
